canonical_definition: A tendency for an evaluator to favor outputs from models in the same family or with similar training patterns.
papers: An Empirical Analysis of Uncertainty in Large Language Model Evaluations
Justice or Prejudice? Quantifying Biases in LLM-as-a-Judge

definitions.text: A tendency for an LLM judge to favor responses generated by itself over responses generated by other models.
